This is my fourth time joining the iThelp Iron Man Contest, which also means I've been doing frontend for almost four years now... (time really flies, RRRR). My theme this time is "A Beginner's Guide to Node.js From Scratch" — why this topic? Honestly just because I wanted to refresh my Node.js knowledge end-to-end, and the Iron Man Contest seemed like a good excuse.
How I Became the 2023 Node.js Software Engineer Enterprise Capstone Program Coach

My company's half-year Node.js Software Engineer Enterprise Capstone Program just wrapped up, so this post records the six-month process and my takeaways. Hopefully it helps future students of this program understand what it's about. Also, this is for my students.

A quick note on building a simple WebSocket chatroom. I'll mainly use Express as the example, with sample code at the end.
A note on deploying Express to Vercel — the steps and the gotchas.
Step-by-Step: Uploading Files to Firebase Storage with Node.js
I previously shared "The Complete Express + Imgur Upload Tutorial", so this time I'm covering Firebase Storage upload. I'll walk through every step clearly because this one's a bit more complex.
Deploy Your Node.js Project to fly.io With flyctl

A walkthrough of deploying an Express app to fly.io. After Heroku killed its free tier, fly.io became one of the alternatives worth trying. This post covers installing flyctl on macOS/Windows/Linux, registering, signing in, deploying with flyctl launch, and checking deploy status. Heads up — fly.io now requires a credit card on file even for the free tier.
Build Your First LINE Bot with Node.js — Using OpenAI GPT-3 and GPT-3.5 as Examples

OpenAI's ChatGPT has been blowing up recently, and OpenAI also exposes an API we can integrate with. This post focuses on GPT-3 but also covers how to switch to ChatGPT API. We'll use Node.js to build a LINE chatbot backed by OpenAI.
Fixing "regeneratorRuntime is not defined" When Using Gulp
A quick note on fixing the `regeneratorRuntime is not defined` issue when using gulp-babel.

(29) The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango - Connecting to Atlas MongoDB
The Frontend Dev Forced to Eat Mango

We already signed up for MongoDB Atlas and played with MongoDB Compass. So the obvious next step is actually wiring it into our own project. In this post I'll show you how little code it takes to point your app at the remote database.
